heuristics.scenarioLabel: Open wider when action folds around toward the right of the bring-in
heuristics.appliesWhen: Comparing seats immediately left of the bring-in against later seats after folds
heuristics.adjustWhen: Immediately left of the bring-in needs a materially stronger start
heuristics.rationale: The source explicitly maps Razz starting-hand quality to how many players remain between you and the bring-in.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: Five-high or better razz starts are premium; six-high is still comfortably playable
heuristics.appliesWhen: Unpaired Razz start whose highest card is five or lower
heuristics.adjustWhen: Six-high starts are usually still playable even from early position
heuristics.rationale: The source gives explicit practical thresholds at five-high, six-high, and seven-high.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: Use visible low-card blockers before continuing with 4-5 down, 7 up style starts
heuristics.appliesWhen: Early-position hand similar to 4-5 down with 7 up
heuristics.adjustWhen: Two exposed blockers makes the spot questionable
heuristics.rationale: The source gives concrete blocker thresholds instead of a vague 'be careful with dead cards' warning.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: Visible opponent cards matter at least as much as your own cards
heuristics.appliesWhen: Any Razz continuation or opening decision where live low cards determine hand quality
heuristics.adjustWhen: Do not evaluate your hand in isolation from opponents' up cards
heuristics.rationale: The source frames visible-card tracking as the difference between losing and winning Razz players.
